patch_description: Knowledge Graph Integration - Data Collection Workflow Additions
instructions: Add these nodes to enable dual-write to Postgres + Neo4j knowledge graph

nodes_to_add.notes: Splits data flow into two parallel paths: Postgres (existing) and Knowledge Graph (new)
Transforms JSONB business data into knowledge graph entity format with attributes and relationships
HTTP POST to Graphiti MCP server. Continue On Fail enabled so Postgres write succeeds even if graph write fails.
nodes_to_add.important: CRITICAL: Enable 'Continue On Fail' in node settings to ensure Postgres write succeeds even if graph is unavailable

nodes_to_add.notes: Recombines Postgres and Knowledge Graph paths before continuing to review insertion

visual_flow.ascii_diagram:
[Combine Business Data]
        |
        v
[Split: Dual Write Path]
        |
    +---+---+
    v       v
[Upsert   [Prepare Graph Entity]
Business]      |
    |     [Write to Knowledge Graph]
    |          |
    +-----+----+
          |
          v
[Merge: After Dual Write]
          |
          v
[Prepare Reviews for Insert]

testing_instructions.step_2: Verify Graphiti MCP server is running: curl http://localhost:3000/health
testing_instructions.step_3: Set Apify URL limit=5 for quick test
testing_instructions.step_5: Check execution log - both 'Upsert Business' and 'Write to Knowledge Graph' should succeed
testing_instructions.step_6: Verify Postgres: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM businesses WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '5 minutes';
testing_instructions.step_7: Verify Neo4j: MATCH (b:Business) WHERE b.last_updated >= datetime() - duration({minutes: 5}) RETURN count(b);
testing_instructions.step_8: Counts should match (5 businesses in both systems)

rollback_instructions.if_issues: To rollback, simply delete the 4 new nodes and reconnect 'Combine Business Data' directly to 'Upsert Business'
rollback_instructions.data_integrity: Existing Postgres data is unaffected. Only new executions will attempt dual-write.
